title: dianthus carthusianorum subsp. tenorei
intro: Dianthus carthusianorum subsp. tenorei, commonly known as the Mary's Tears or Tenore's Pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Dianthus carthusianorum subsp. tenorei include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
